extra.message: User input from request.args.get('name') is passed to template and rendered with the | safe filter, which disables auto-escaping. This allows reflected XSS attacks via the 'name' GET parameter.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: User input from request.cookies.get('name') is passed to template and rendered with the | safe filter, which disables auto-escaping. This allows reflected XSS attacks via the 'name' cookie.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: Message content from the database is rendered with the | safe filter on lines 29-31, disabling auto-escaping. Messages are stored without sanitization in send_feedback() endpoint (account_area/views.py:75), allowing stored XSS attacks.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: The 'next' parameter from request.args is used directly in redirect() without validation. An attacker can pass arbitrary URLs (e.g., ?next=http://evil.com) to perform open redirect attacks.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site
